channel.title: BUbreakingSectionTwo
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from BUbreakingSectionTwo, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Starmer’s Chief of Staff Resigns, Citing Role in Hiring Friend of Epstein
item.description: The Labour official was ensnared in a scandal after helping appoint Peter Mandelson as Britain’s ambassador to the U.S.

item.title: Russian attacks on Ukraine’s energy infrastructure are the biggest threat to its economy, which could shrink as much as 3%
item.description: The cost of purchasing and operating generators to overcome power outages is just one of many challenges facing Ukrainian businesses after nearly four...

item.title: The Super Bowl was made for TV and instant replay was made for visual AI. Here’s how it could be better and what it would look like
item.description: Visual AI can make the big game — and every Sunday — better.

item.title: Minnesotans say immigration agents are impersonating construction workers, delivery drivers and anti-ICE activists
item.description: Local officials also say ICE agents have been seen swapping license plates or using bogus ones, a violation of state law.

item.title: Washington Post C.E.O. Will Lewis Steps Down After Stormy Tenure
item.description: His departure came days after the company cut 30 percent of the staff. He will be replaced in the interim by Jeff D’Onofrio, the chief financial offic...

item.title: All the things you wanted to know about Super Bowl rings but were afraid to ask
item.description: One year's bling cost an estimated $36,500 each.

item.title: Washington Post publisher to step down after big layoffs as union calls his legacy ‘attempted destruction of a great American journalism institution’
item.description: His tenure has been rocky from the start, marked by layoffs and a failed reorganization plan that led to the departure of former top editor Sally Buzb...

item.title: The Secret History of the Deep State
item.description: Seven pages of grand jury testimony from Richard Nixon were deemed so incendiary that they were hidden from the public for years.

item.title: A First Look Below Antarctica’s Most Menacing Glacier
item.description: Scientists on the Thwaites Glacier attempted to install equipment beneath the ice to better understand how it is melting. Our climate reporter Raymond...

item.title: Breezy Johnson’s celebration is muted by Lindsey Vonn’s crash.
item.description: Johnson won her first Olympic gold medal after her teammate Vonn crashed early in her run.

item.title: Live Updates: Japan’s Leader Set for Big Win in Snap Election
item.description: Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi was on course for a sweeping mandate from voters for her economic policies and tough stance on immigration and China.

item.title: Portugal Votes for President, With Leftist Set to Beat Surging Far Right
item.description: Polls predict a comfortable victory for António José Seguro, but a nationalist’s presence in the runoff showed that Portugal is not immune to Europe’s...

item.title: Starmer’s Chief of Staff Resigns, Citing Role in Hiring Friend of Epstein
item.description: The Labour official was ensnared in a scandal after helping appoint Peter Mandelson as Britain’s ambassador to the U.S.

item.title: Savannah Guthrie, in New Video, Promises to Pay for Her Mother’s Return
item.description: The “Today” show anchor, in a message on social media with her siblings, said the return of their mother Nancy “is the only way we will have peace.”
